{"notes":"## Postmortem (2026-03-21T10:58:38Z)

ROOT CAUSE: The automated agent ran for 98 episodes across 2 iterations but produced zero successful tool calls (tools=0 in the OTel summary), meaning it never actually read or modified any files. The agent appears to have been stuck in a feedback/retry loop without executing any substantive coding actions, resulting in a 100% stuck rate and 0% success rate.

PATTERN: This is a classic "stuck agent" failure pattern — the orchestrator spawned agents that exhausted their iteration budget posting TensorZero feedback metrics in a tight loop (~65ms per episode) rather than invoking file-editing tools. The agents=2, tools=0 signature confirms no actual work was done.

FIX STRATEGY:
- Start by explicitly reading the target files before attempting any edits: use read_file on coordination/src/agent_profile.rs and coordination/src/tool_registry.rs (or equivalent tool_*.rs file) to understand current struct definitions and trait implementations before writing any changes.
- Constrain the task to a single focused subtask per agent invocation — do not attempt to review and update both files in one pass; complete agent_profile.rs first, verify it compiles, then move to the tool registry file.
- After each file edit, run the compiler (cargo check or cargo build limited to the coordination crate) and parse the output to confirm no regressions before proceeding, rather than relying on the orchestrator's success signal.

REVISED DESCRIPTION:

Subtask 3: Update AgentProfile and tool registry types in the coordination crate

Context: This is a focused Rust code update task. Two files need to be reviewed and updated to align with new interface requirements. Do not attempt to modify any other files.

Target files (in order):
  1. coordination/src/agent_profile.rs
  2. coordination/src/tool_registry.rs (check if named tool_catalog.rs or tools.rs if not found at this path)

Step-by-step instructions:

Step 1 — Read agent_profile.rs in full. Identify the AgentProfile struct and any associated impl blocks or trait implementations. Note every field name and type.

Step 2 — Read tool_registry.rs (or equivalent) in full. Identify the ToolRegistry struct or enum, its public methods, and any trait bounds it requires from AgentProfile.

Step 3 — Read the parent module file (coordination/src/lib.rs) to understand what is publicly exported and what downstream crates consume from these two modules.

Step 4 — Make targeted edits to agent_profile.rs only. Specific changes required:
  - Ensure AgentProfile derives or implements Clone, Debug, and serde::Serialize/Deserialize if not already present.
  - Add or update the capabilities field to use Vec<String> if it currently uses a custom type that is no longer defined elsewhere.
  - If there is a builder pattern (AgentProfileBuilder), ensure the build() method returns Result<AgentProfile, ConfigError> not a bare AgentProfile.

Step 5 — Run: cargo check -p coordination 2>&1 and read the full output. Fix any errors in agent_profile.rs before proceeding.

Step 6 — Make targeted edits to tool_registry.rs only. Specific changes required:
  - Ensure ToolRegistry::register() accepts &mut self, tool_name: &str, handler: ToolHandler where ToolHandler is the type alias already defined in this file.
  - Ensure ToolRegistry::lookup() returns Option<&ToolHandler> not a cloned value.
  - Remove any reference to AgentCapability enum if it has been replaced by String-based capabilities in agent_profile.rs.

Step 7 — Run: cargo check -p coordination 2>&1 again. Fix any remaining errors.

Step 8 — Run: cargo test -p coordination 2>&1. If tests fail, read the test file at coordination/tests/ or coordination/src/tests.rs and fix only the test breakage caused by the struct changes above, not unrelated test failures.

Step 9 — Confirm the final state by reading back both modified files and summarizing what changed.

Do not modify any files outside the coordination crate. Do not attempt to update Cargo.toml unless a dependency is genuinely missing and cargo check explicitly reports it. Do not loop on feedback posting — if a step fails, read the error output and fix it directly."}

{"notes":"## Postmortem (2026-03-21T11:00:38Z)

ROOT CAUSE: The agent ran for nearly 2 hours (6724 seconds) across only 2 iterations and 143 parallel episodes, all of which failed to resolve the task. The 100% stuck rate and 0% success rate indicate the agent never made meaningful progress on the actual file edit — it likely got into a loop reading the file or attempting edits that never compiled or were never committed.

PATTERN: This is a stuck/loop failure pattern combined with likely compilation cascade — the agent repeatedly attempted to modify crates/swarm-agents/src/config.rs but either produced edits that failed to compile (causing it to retry indefinitely) or failed to locate the correct edit targets, burning all wall time without a single successful verification pass.

FIX STRATEGY:
- Read the exact current contents of crates/swarm-agents/src/config.rs first before attempting any edit, then make one targeted, minimal change rather than attempting a full rewrite; confirm the file compiles with `cargo check -p swarm-agents` before moving on.
- Do not attempt to infer struct field names or default values from memory — use grep or cat to find the exact field names, types, and impl blocks already present in config.rs and any related files (e.g., crates/swarm-agents/src/lib.rs, Cargo.toml) before writing any replacement code.
- After each edit, run `cargo check -p swarm-agents` and if it fails, read the compiler error output in full before retrying; do not loop on the same edit more than twice without changing approach.

REVISED DESCRIPTION:

Task: Update configuration in crates/swarm-agents/src/config.rs

Target file: crates/swarm-agents/src/config.rs

Step-by-step instructions:

Step 1 — Reconnaissance (do this before any edit):
  Run: cat crates/swarm-agents/src/config.rs
  Also run: grep -rn "Config\|config" crates/swarm-agents/src/ --include="*.rs" -l
  Record the exact struct name(s), field names, field types, and any impl blocks or Default implementations already present.

Step 2 — Identify what needs to change:
  The issue requires ensuring the configuration struct and its defaults are correct and complete. Specifically:
  - Verify that all required fields are present with appropriate types (check any usage sites in crates/swarm-agents/src/ to find what fields are actually consumed).
  - Verify that Default::default() or a constructor function returns sensible values for each field.
  - If any field is missing, add it with the correct type and a documented default.
  - If any field has a wrong type or default, correct it.

Step 3 — Make the edit:
  Use a precise, surgical edit targeting only the lines that need to change. Do not rewrite the entire file. Preserve all existing doc comments, derives, and imports unless they are specifically wrong.

Step 4 — Verify compilation immediately:
  Run: cargo check -p swarm-agents
  If this fails, read the full compiler error, fix only what the compiler reports, and re-check. Do not proceed to the next step until cargo check passes.

Step 5 — Run any existing tests:
  Run: cargo test -p swarm-agents 2>&1 | head -60
  Fix any test failures introduced by the config change.

Step 6 — Confirm and close:
  Confirm the file diff is minimal and correct, then mark the issue resolved.

Important constraints:
  - Do not spend more than 2 tool calls on reconnaissance before making the first edit.
  - Do not retry the same failing edit more than once without reading the compiler error and changing the approach.
  - If cargo check fails after 3 attempts, stop and report the exact compiler error rather than continuing to loop."}

subtask-3: Review and update coordination and orchestration files

Context: This task is part of a larger swarm effort. Previous automated attempts failed because agents looped without making committed progress. Follow these steps exactly.

Step 1 — Discover what exists:
Run: ls -la coordination/
Run: ls -la coordination/orchestration/ (if it exists)
Read every file you find before touching anything. Do not assume file names.

Step 2 — Identify the specific files that need updating:
Look for files matching these patterns in the coordination/ directory:
  - Any file named subtask-3.* or *subtask*3*
  - Any file named orchestration.*, swarm.*, or agents.*
  - Any README or STATUS file

Step 3 — Make the minimal required changes:
For each file that needs updating, do the following sequence:
  a. Read the current file content in full
  b. Identify exactly which lines/sections need to change (status fields, agent assignments, timestamps, dependency references)
  c. Write the updated file
  d. Read the file back to confirm the write succeeded
  e. Commit with message: "subtask-3: update [filename] coordination state"

Step 4 — Update the orchestration registry:
If there is a central orchestration file (e.g., coordination/orchestration.toml, coordination/swarm.yaml, or similar), update the entry for subtask-3 to reflect status=complete or status=in-progress as appropriate. Commit separately.

Step 5 — Do not proceed to the next file until the current file is committed. One file per commit.

Acceptance criteria:
- All files in coordination/ that reference subtask-3 have been updated with current status
- Each change is in its own commit with a descriptive message
- No file is left in a partially-edited state
- The task is marked done after all commits are pushed

{"notes":"## Postmortem (2026-03-21T19:16:17Z)

ROOT CAUSE: Both subtask workers exhausted their context budget doing reconnaissance (11 tool calls total, all reads and searches, zero writes) and then received empty responses from the model endpoint, likely because the conversation grew too long before any edit was attempted. The baseline verification then revealed the worktree already fails fmt/clippy/check before any agent changes, meaning the codebase was broken before the run started.

PATTERN: This is a read-loop-without-write failure combined with a broken-baseline abort — the agents spent all their turns gathering information instead of making the single targeted edit, then the orchestrator correctly refused to proceed because the pre-existing build was already red.

Fix Strategy:
- Fix the baseline build failure first, before retrying this issue. The orchestrator log shows 0/3 gates passed (fmt:FAIL) on the unmodified worktree. Run cargo fmt, cargo clippy --fix, and cargo check in crates/swarm-agents and confirm all three pass on a clean branch before dispatching any agent. An agent cannot succeed when the baseline is broken because every verification loop will fail regardless of what the agent writes.
- The function default_actor() already exists in beads_bridge.rs at lines 370-400 (the search results prove this — the agent found it at line 370 with the correct three-step fallback chain). The Rust implementation work is already done. The next attempt should skip subtask-1 entirely and instead verify that all call sites in orchestrator/mod.rs and any other files have been updated to call default_actor() instead of inlining the env var read. The only remaining work is confirming no duplicate inline reads remain and that cargo build and cargo test pass.
- Do not use concurrent subtask dispatch for this task. The planner incorrectly split a single-file change into two parallel workers. Run a single sequential agent scoped only to crates/swarm-agents/src/beads_bridge.rs with a strict instruction to make at most two tool calls (one read to confirm the current state, one write if any inline BD_ACTOR read outside default_actor() still exists) and then immediately run cargo build and cargo test.

REVISED DESCRIPTION:

Title: Verify and complete the BD_ACTOR default_actor() extraction in beads_bridge.rs

Context: A previous automated attempt found that pub fn default_actor() -> String already exists in crates/swarm-agents/src/beads_bridge.rs at approximately line 370. The function implements the correct three-step fallback: BD_ACTOR env var, then git config user.name via Command, then hostname -s via Command, then hard-coded "worker". The Rust implementation is present. What is unknown is whether the baseline build is passing and whether any duplicate inline BD_ACTOR reads remain outside that function.

Pre-flight — mandatory before touching any source file:
From the repo root, run these three commands in sequence and confirm each exits with code 0:
  cargo fmt --check -p swarm-agents
  cargo clippy -p swarm-agents -- -D warnings
  cargo check -p swarm-agents
If any of these fail, fix the pre-existing failures first. Do not proceed until all three pass on the unmodified branch. The orchestrator will abort the entire run if the baseline is broken, making all agent work irrelevant.

Step 1 — Confirm the function exists and is correct:
Open crates/swarm-agents/src/beads_bridge.rs. Read lines 355 through 410. Confirm that pub fn default_actor() -> String is present at module scope, is marked pub, and implements exactly: (a) std::env::var("BD_ACTOR") check with trim and empty guard, (b) Command::new("git").args(["config", "user.name"]).output() fallback, (c) Command::new("hostname").arg("-s").output() fallback, (d) "worker".to_string() as the final fallback. If the function is missing or incorrect, insert or correct it now before proceeding.

Step 2 — Find all remaining inline BD_ACTOR reads:
Search the entire crates/ directory for the literal string BD_ACTOR. {"notes":"## Postmortem (2026-03-21T20:35:05Z)

ROOT CAUSE: The orchestrator aborted before any agent touched source code because cargo fmt --check failed on the unmodified worktree, meaning the codebase had pre-existing formatting violations that caused the baseline verification gate to return 0/3 passed. The subtask planner also failed to parse its own JSON output, so the run never even reached the agent dispatch phase.

PATTERN: Broken-baseline abort combined with planner JSON parse failure — the agent system correctly refused to proceed when the pre-flight gates were red, but no one fixed the underlying fmt violations before dispatching the run.

Fix Strategy:
- Before dispatching any agent, run cargo fmt -p swarm-agents (not --check, the fixing variant) in the worktree, commit the result, and confirm cargo fmt --check -p swarm-agents then exits 0. Only after that commit should the orchestrator be invoked.
- Disable concurrent subtask dispatch for this issue entirely. The postmortem already identified this as the wrong strategy for a single-file change, and the planner's JSON parse failure confirms the subtask planning step is unreliable here. Use a single sequential worker scoped to crates/swarm-agents/src/beads_bridge.rs with a hard cap of four tool calls total.
- The Rust implementation work (pub fn default_actor()) is already done per the previous run's search results. The worker's only job is: read beads_bridge.rs lines 355-410 to confirm the function exists, grep the crates/ directory for any std::env::var("BD_ACTOR") outside that function body, replace any found with a call to default_actor(), then run cargo build -p swarm-agents and cargo test -p swarm-agents. If no inline reads are found outside the function, the worker should make zero edits and just confirm the build passes.

REVISED DESCRIPTION:

Title: Verify and complete the BD_ACTOR default_actor() extraction in beads_bridge.rs

Background: A pub fn default_actor() -> String already exists in crates/swarm-agents/src/beads_bridge.rs at approximately line 370. It implements the correct three-step fallback: BD_ACTOR env var, then git config user.name, then hostname -s, then the hard-coded string "worker". The Rust implementation is present. Two things are unknown: whether the baseline build is clean, and whether any duplicate inline BD_ACTOR reads remain outside that function.

Mandatory pre-flight before touching any source file:

Step 0 — Fix formatting first. From the repo root, run:
  cargo fmt -p swarm-agents
Then run:
  cargo fmt --check -p swarm-agents
Confirm the check exits 0. If it does not, the fmt run did not fix everything — inspect the output and fix remaining issues manually. Do not proceed until fmt --check exits 0. Commit the formatting fix as a standalone commit with message "chore: cargo fmt swarm-agents" before doing anything else.

Step 1 — Run the full baseline gate. After the fmt commit, run in sequence:
  cargo clippy -p swarm-agents -- -D warnings
  cargo check -p swarm-agents
Both must exit 0. If clippy fails, fix each warning it reports (do not suppress with allow attributes unless the warning is a known false positive already present in the codebase). Commit any clippy fixes separately before proceeding.

Step 2 — Confirm default_actor() exists and is correct. Open crates/swarm-agents/src/beads_bridge.rs and read lines 355 through 415. Confirm all of the following are true:
  a) pub fn default_actor() -> String is present at module scope (not inside an impl block).
  b) The first check is std::env::var("BD_ACTOR") or env::var("BD_ACTOR"), with a trim and an empty-string guard so a blank env var falls through.
  c) The second fallback runs Command::new("git").args(["config", "user.name"]).output() and uses stdout if the command succeeds and the output is non-empty after trimming.
  d) The third fallback runs Command::new("hostname").arg("-s").output() and uses stdout if the command succeeds and the output is non-empty after trimming.
  e) The final fallback returns "worker".to_string().
If any of these conditions is false, correct the function now. The corrected signature must be exactly: pub fn default_actor() -> String

Step 3 — Find all remaining inline BD_ACTOR reads. Run:
  grep -rn 'BD_ACTOR' crates/
Examine every line in the output. The only acceptable occurrences are:
  a) Lines inside the body of default_actor() itself.
  b) Lines in comments or string literals that mention the env var by name for documentation purposes.
Any line containing std::env::var("BD_ACTOR") or env::var("BD_ACTOR") in executable code outside the body of default_actor() is a bug. For each such line, replace the entire expression — including any .unwrap_or, .unwrap_or_else, or chained fallback logic — with a single call to default_actor() if the call site is in beads_bridge.rs, or crate::beads_bridge::default_actor() if the call site is in another module. Add a use import if needed.

Step 4 — Check orchestrator/mod.rs and driver.rs specifically. Open crates/swarm-agents/src/orchestrator/mod.rs and search for BD_ACTOR. Open crates/swarm-agents/src/driver.rs and search for BD_ACTOR. Previous runs found no hits in driver.rs and no functional hits in orchestrator/mod.rs, but confirm this is still true on the current branch. If either file has an inline env var read, replace it as described in Step 3.

Step 5 — Build and test. Run:
  cargo build -p swarm-agents
Fix any compilation errors before continuing. Then run:
  cargo test -p swarm-agents
All tests must pass. Pay attention to any test that sets BD_ACTOR in the environment — default_actor() must honor it as the first priority.

Step 6 — Fix scripts/run-swarm.sh. Open scripts/run-swarm.sh and go to line 98. Read the enclosing function or loop block. If the variable _prev_arg is read before it is assigned anywhere in that block, insert the line:
  _prev_arg=""
as the very first line of that block. Then run:
  bash -n scripts/run-swarm.sh
Confirm it exits 0 with no output. Then run the script with no arguments and confirm it exits 0 with no "unbound variable" error.

Acceptance criteria (all must be true before closing this issue):
- pub fn default_actor() -> String exists at module scope in crates/swarm-agents/src/beads_bridge.rs.
- The function is the only place in the codebase where std::env::var("BD_ACTOR") appears in executable code.
- cargo fmt --check -p swarm-agents exits 0.
- cargo clippy -p swarm-agents -- -D warnings exits 0.
- cargo build -p swarm-agents exits 0.
- cargo test -p swarm-agents exits 0 with all tests passing.
- bash -n scripts/run-swarm.sh exits 0.
- scripts/run-swarm.sh with no arguments exits 0 with no unbound variable error.

Dispatch instructions for the orchestrator:
- Do not use concurrent subtask dispatch. Use a single sequential worker.
- Scope the worker to crates/swarm-agents/ and scripts/run-swarm.sh only.
- The worker must run cargo fmt -p swarm-agents as its very first action, before reading any source file.
- If the baseline gate (fmt --check, clippy, check) fails after the fmt run, the worker must fix the pre-existing issue before making any issue-related edits. This is not optional.
- The worker should make at most one commit for the fmt fix, one commit for any clippy fixes, and one commit for the actual default_actor() cleanup. Three commits maximum."}

PATTERN: This is a stuck-loop / feedback-storm pattern where the orchestrator's episode-management machinery consumed all available iterations posting metrics to TensorZero, leaving zero capacity for the actual coding worker to dispatch and execute tool calls against the codebase.

FIX STRATEGY:
- Cap TensorZero feedback posting to a single batch call after the worker finishes, not one call per episode per metric; the 103-episode × 3-metric fan-out is what ate the iteration budget and caused the stuck-rate SLO to hit 1.00.
- Dispatch a single coding worker with an explicit first action of running cargo fmt -p swarm-agents (the mutating variant, not --check) before touching any gate or source file, then commit the result as "chore: cargo fmt swarm-agents" — this is the pre-existing formatting violation that caused the original baseline gate to score 0/3 and must be resolved before any verification runs.
- Scope the worker to exactly two files (crates/swarm-agents/src/beads_bridge.rs and scripts/run-swarm.sh) with a hard tool-call cap of 8, and require it to produce a git diff showing changes before it is allowed to call any verification gate.

REVISED DESCRIPTION:

Title: Extract BD_ACTOR default logic into pub fn default_actor() in beads_bridge.rs

Background: The env var BD_ACTOR is read inline in multiple places inside crates/swarm-agents/src/. The goal is to consolidate all reads into a single pub fn default_actor() -> String at module scope in crates/swarm-agents/src/beads_bridge.rs, implementing the fallback chain: BD_ACTOR env var (trimmed, non-empty) -> git config user.name (via Command::new) -> hostname -s (via Command::new) -> hard-coded "worker".

There are two known pre-existing blockers that must be resolved before any issue-related edit:
1. cargo fmt --check -p swarm-agents exits non-zero due to formatting violations unrelated to this task.
2. The subtask planner has a documented JSON parse failure when given concurrent workers; use exactly one sequential worker.

Step 0 — Fix formatting first, before reading any source file or running any gate:
  Run: cargo fmt -p swarm-agents
  Then run: cargo fmt --check -p swarm-agents
  If check exits 0, commit: git commit -am "chore: cargo fmt swarm-agents"
  Do not proceed past this step until the check exits 0.

Step 1 — Confirm baseline compiles:
  Run: cargo clippy -p swarm-agents -- -D warnings
  Run: cargo check -p swarm-agents
  Both must exit 0. Fix any pre-existing clippy warnings without using allow attributes. Commit fixes as "fix: pre-existing clippy warnings" if any exist.

Step 2 — Audit all BD_ACTOR reads:
  Run: grep -rn 'BD_ACTOR' crates/
  List every file and line number. The only acceptable occurrences after this task are inside the body of default_actor() in beads_bridge.rs, or in comments/string literals. Pay special attention to:
    crates/swarm-agents/src/beads_bridge.rs (the target file)
    crates/swarm-agents/src/orchestrator/mod.rs
    crates/swarm-agents/src/driver.rs

Step 3 — Ensure pub fn default_actor() -> String exists correctly in beads_bridge.rs:
  Open crates/swarm-agents/src/beads_bridge.rs and read lines 355-415.
  The function must be at module scope (not inside an impl block).
  The exact signature must be: pub fn default_actor() -> String
  The body must implement this fallback chain in order:
    a) std::env::var("BD_ACTOR").unwrap_or_default(), trim whitespace, return if non-empty.
    b) Command::new("git").args(["config", "user.name"]).output(), use stdout trimmed if non-empty.
    c) Command::new("hostname").arg("-s").output(), use stdout trimmed if non-empty.
    d) Return "worker".to_string() as the final fallback.
  If the function is missing or incorrect, write it. If it already exists and is correct, make no change.

Step 4 — Replace all inline BD_ACTOR reads outside default_actor():
  For each line found in Step 2 that contains env::var("BD_ACTOR") or std::env::var("BD_ACTOR") in executable code outside the body of default_actor():
    If the call site is in beads_bridge.rs: replace the entire expression (including any chained .unwrap_or / .unwrap_or_else fallback logic) with a single call to default_actor().
    If the call site is in another module: replace with crate::beads_bridge::default_actor() and add a use import if needed.
  Make all replacements in a single commit: "feat: consolidate BD_ACTOR reads into default_actor()".

Step 5 — Fix scripts/run-swarm.sh unbound variable:
  Open scripts/run-swarm.sh and find the block around line 98.
  If _prev_arg is referenced before assignment anywhere in that block, insert _prev_arg="" as the first line of the block.
  Run: bash -n scripts/run-swarm.sh
  Confirm it exits 0 with no output.
  Run the script with no arguments and confirm it exits 0 with no unbound variable error.

Step 6 — Build and test:
  Run: cargo build -p swarm-agents
  Fix any compilation errors before proceeding.
  Run: cargo test -p swarm-agents
  All tests must pass. If any test sets BD_ACTOR in the environment, default_actor() must return that value as the first priority.

Acceptance criteria (all must be true before closing):
  pub fn default_actor() -> String exists at module scope in crates/swarm-agents/src/beads_bridge.rs.
  grep -rn 'BD_ACTOR' crates/ shows occurrences only inside the body of default_actor() or in comments.
  cargo fmt --check -p swarm-agents exits 0.
  cargo clippy -p swarm-agents -- -D warnings exits 0.
  cargo build -p swarm-agents exits 0.
  cargo test -p swarm-agents exits 0 with all tests passing.
  bash -n scripts/run-swarm.sh exits 0.
  scripts/run-swarm.sh with no arguments exits 0 with no unbound variable error.

Dispatch constraints:
  One sequential worker only. No concurrent subtask dispatch.
  Worker scope: crates/swarm-agents/ and scripts/run-swarm.sh only.
  First action must be cargo fmt -p swarm-agents. No exceptions.
  Hard cap: 8 tool calls total.
  Worker must produce a non-empty git diff before calling any verification gate.
  TensorZero feedback must be posted once in a single batch after the worker exits, not per-episode per-metric."}

FIX STRATEGY:

Title: Add unit test for BeadsBridge::poll_mail_inbox verifying empty inbox returns None

Context and file location:
The implementation lives in src/beads_bridge.rs (crate: beefcake-swarm). The function under test is BeadsBridge::poll_mail_inbox. It shells out to the binary path stored in the environment variable SWARM_BEADS_BIN and parses stdout to find waiting mail messages. When the binary produces no output (empty stdout), poll_mail_inbox must return None.

Pre-conditions (verify before writing any code):

Add a span so molecule creation appears in structured logs.","notes":"## Postmortem (2026-03-23T14:41:29Z)

ROOT CAUSE: The automated agent never got to make any changes because the baseline verification step failed — the repository was already in a broken state (fmt check failing) before any modifications were attempted. The agent correctly aborted rather than waste iterations on a pre-broken codebase.

PATTERN: This is a pre-existing compilation/formatting failure pattern. The worktree failed 0/3 gates (fmt:FAIL → clippy:SKIP → check:SKIP), meaning the codebase had unformatted code or syntax errors before the agent touched anything. This is not an agent logic failure but a dirty baseline problem.

FIX STRATEGY:
- Before re-running the agent, manually run `cargo fmt` in the repository root and commit the result so the baseline passes the fmt gate. The agent's gate check requires fmt, clippy, and check to all pass before it will proceed.
- Confirm the baseline is clean by running `cargo fmt --check && cargo clippy -- -D warnings && cargo check` locally and verifying all three pass. Only then re-trigger the automated run.
- The actual code change needed is straightforward: in `src/subtask.rs` (or wherever `create_molecule_for_plan` lives), add `#[tracing::instrument]` above the function signature and ensure `tracing` is already in `Cargo.toml` as a dependency (it almost certainly is given the codebase uses structured logging). No architectural changes are needed.

REVISED DESCRIPTION:

Title: Add tracing span to create_molecule_for_plan for observability

Pre-condition check (do this first, before any code edits):
Run `cargo fmt` and `cargo check` from the repo root. If either fails, fix and commit those issues before proceeding. The automated harness will abort if the baseline is broken.

Target file: src/subtask.rs (confirm exact path with `grep -r "create_molecule_for_plan" --include="*.rs" .`)

Target function signature (locate it): `fn create_molecule_for_plan(` — this is likely an async fn or a plain fn that constructs a Molecule struct from a plan.

Step 1: Verify `tracing` is listed as a dependency in Cargo.toml. If not, add `tracing = "0.1"` under [dependencies].

Step 2: Add the attribute macro directly above the `fn create_molecule_for_plan` definition:

    #[tracing::instrument]

If the function takes arguments you want captured in the span, use the fields form:

    #[tracing::instrument(fields(plan_id = ?plan.id))]

Adjust field names to match the actual parameter names in the function signature.

Step 3: Run `cargo fmt` to ensure formatting is clean, then `cargo clippy` and `cargo check` to confirm no new errors were introduced.

Step 4: Commit with message: "Add tracing::instrument to create_molecule_for_plan for observability"

Do NOT modify any other functions or files. This is a single-attribute addition. {"notes":"## Postmortem (2026-03-23T14:44:38Z)

ROOT CAUSE: The automated agent aborted before making any changes because the baseline fmt gate failed — the repository had unformatted Rust code already present before the agent touched anything. Since the harness requires all three gates (fmt, clippy, check) to pass as a precondition, it correctly refused to proceed rather than burn iterations on a dirty baseline.

PATTERN: This is a dirty baseline / pre-existing formatting failure pattern. The worktree failed 0/3 gates (fmt:FAIL → clippy:SKIP → check:SKIP), which is a known abort condition in the harness, not an agent logic error or edit mismatch.

FIX STRATEGY:
- Manually run `cargo fmt` in the repository root right now, review the diff to confirm it only touches whitespace/formatting (no logic changes), and commit the result with message "cargo fmt: fix pre-existing formatting" before re-triggering the agent.
- After committing the fmt fix, verify the baseline is clean by running `cargo fmt --check && cargo clippy -- -D warnings && cargo check` locally and confirming all three exit 0. Do not re-trigger the agent until this passes.
- The actual code change is a one-line attribute addition above `create_molecule_for_plan` in src/subtask.rs — confirm the exact path with `grep -rn "fn create_molecule_for_plan" --include="*.rs" .` and add `#[tracing::instrument]` (or with fields if useful) directly above the fn keyword. No other files need to change unless `tracing` is absent from Cargo.toml, which is unlikely.

REVISED DESCRIPTION:

Title: Add tracing::instrument to create_molecule_for_plan for observability

Pre-condition (mandatory before any code edits):

The previous automated run failed entirely because the repository had unformatted code before the agent started. Fix this first.

Step 0 — Fix the baseline:
Run `cargo fmt` from the repository root. Commit the result immediately with message "cargo fmt: fix pre-existing formatting". Then run `cargo fmt --check && cargo clippy -- -D warnings && cargo check` and confirm all three commands exit 0. Do not proceed to Step 1 until this is confirmed clean. If clippy or check also fail after fmt, fix those issues and commit them separately before continuing.

Step 1 — Locate the target function:
Run `grep -rn "fn create_molecule_for_plan" --include="*.rs" .` from the repo root. The postmortem identifies src/subtask.rs as the expected location. Confirm the exact file path and line number. Also note the full function signature (parameters and their names/types) because you will need them for the instrument fields.

Step 2 — Verify tracing dependency:
Open Cargo.toml and search for `tracing`. If it is already listed under [dependencies], no change is needed. If it is absent (unlikely given the codebase uses structured logging), add `tracing = "0.1"` under [dependencies] and run `cargo check` to confirm it resolves.

Step 3 — Add the attribute:
Open the file identified in Step 1. Directly above the line that begins `fn create_molecule_for_plan(` (or `pub fn`, `async fn`, `pub async fn` — whatever the exact signature is), add exactly one line:

    #[tracing::instrument]

If the function takes a plan parameter and you want the plan identifier captured in the span, use the fields form instead:

    #[tracing::instrument(fields(plan_id = ?plan.id))]

Replace `plan.id` with the actual field path that makes sense given the real parameter names. If you are unsure, use the plain `#[tracing::instrument]` form without fields — it is sufficient for the observability goal stated in the issue.

Do not modify any other functions. Do not reformat surrounding code. Do not add use statements unless the compiler requires them (it will not, since the attribute uses the full path `tracing::instrument`).

Step 4 — Verify:
Run `cargo fmt --check` to confirm formatting is still clean. Run `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` and `cargo check` to confirm no new errors. If clippy warns about the instrument macro (e.g., about skipping a non-Debug parameter), either derive Debug on the relevant type or add `#[tracing::instrument(skip(param_name))]` to suppress it.

Step 5 — Commit:
Commit only src/subtask.rs (and Cargo.toml if you had to add the tracing dependency) with message: "Add tracing::instrument to create_molecule_for_plan for observability"

Scope constraint: This is a single-attribute addition. The only files that should appear in the final diff are src/subtask.rs and optionally Cargo.toml. If the diff is larger than that, something went wrong."}

{"notes":"## Postmortem (2026-03-23T21:34:17Z)

ROOT CAUSE: The worker subtasks all failed immediately with HTTP 502 because TensorZero has no registered provider for the function "tensorzero::function_name::worker_code_edit" — the routing table is missing an entry for that function name, so every subtask call was dead on arrival before any code was touched. Compounding this, the baseline verification then confirmed the worktree itself does not compile (0/3 gates: fmt fails, clippy and check skipped), meaning even if the routing were fixed, the agent would have been dropped into a broken starting state.

PATTERN: This is a TensorZero function routing misconfiguration combined with a pre-existing compilation failure in the worktree — the subtask dispatch infrastructure is broken at the provider layer, not at the code-editing logic layer. It is a known "infrastructure not ready" failure pattern, distinct from edit_file mismatch or model hallucination.

FIX STRATEGY:
- Before re-running the agent, register the missing TensorZero function route: add a "worker_code_edit" function entry in the TensorZero config (typically tensorzero.toml or the equivalent gateway config) pointing to a valid cloud provider (e.g., claude-opus-4-6 or gemini-3-flash-preview), matching the exact function name "tensorzero::function_name::worker_code_edit" that the subtask runtime resolves against.
- Fix the baseline compilation failure in the worktree before the agent touches anything: run "cargo fmt --check" in crates/swarm-agents, resolve the formatting violations manually, confirm "cargo check" and "cargo clippy" pass clean on the main branch, then re-create the worktree from that clean baseline so the 3/3 gate check passes before any agent iteration begins.
- Simplify the task to a single-file sequential edit rather than a 3-way concurrent subtask dispatch: the actual work is small (update one or two constants in config.rs and one environment variable reference), so dispatching three concurrent workers to config.rs, migration_matrix.rs, and fs_tools.rs is over-engineered for this change and multiplies the blast radius of any infrastructure fault.

REVISED DESCRIPTION:

Deploy Qwen3-Coder-Next on vasp-03, replacing Qwen2.5-27B-Opus-Distilled as the fast/scout model.

Prerequisites (must be confirmed before agent runs):
1. Confirm TensorZero gateway has a valid provider registered for function name "worker_code_edit". Check /home/brian/code/beefcake-swarm/tensorzero.toml (or equivalent config path). The function block must exist and must map to a reachable cloud model. If missing, add it and restart the TensorZero gateway before proceeding.
2. Confirm the main branch compiles clean: cd /home/brian/code/beefcake-swarm && cargo fmt && cargo check -p swarm-agents && cargo clippy -p swarm-agents. All three must pass before creating the worktree.
3. Confirm vasp-03 is running the new model: curl http://vasp-03:8081/v1/models should return Qwen3-Coder-Next. If not, run: sbatch run-27b-256k.slurm (note: the slurm script name may need updating — see step below).

Code changes required (sequential, single agent, do not parallelize):

Step 1 — Update crates/swarm-agents/src/config.rs:
- Find the constant or environment variable binding for SWARM_FAST_MODEL. It will look like: const SWARM_FAST_MODEL: &str = "..." or a dotenv/env lookup with a default string containing "27B-Opus-Distilled" or similar.
- Change the default value to "Qwen3-Coder-Next".
- Find any hardcoded model name strings in the fast-endpoint health-check hint message (the ERROR log currently says 'model=Qwen3-Coder-Next' which suggests this is already partially updated, but verify the sbatch hint still references run-27b-256k.slurm — if so, update that hint string to reference the correct new slurm script name for Qwen3-Coder-Next).
- Do not touch migration_matrix.rs or fs_tools.rs unless grep confirms those files contain a literal string matching the old model name "27B-Opus-Distilled" or "Opus-Distilled". Check first with: grep -r "Opus-Distilled\|27B-Opus\|FAST_MODEL" crates/swarm-agents/src/

Step 2 — Verify and commit:
- Run cargo fmt -p swarm-agents
- Run cargo check -p swarm-agents
- Run cargo clippy -p swarm-agents -- -D warnings
- All must pass before committing. Commit message: "deploy: replace 27B-Opus-Distilled with Qwen3-Coder-Next on vasp-03"

Step 3 — Environment variable:
- Update the SWARM_FAST_MODEL entry in the deployment environment file (check .env, systemd unit, or slurm job script used to launch the swarm-agents process on the control node) to match the new model name.

Do not attempt to update migration_matrix.rs or fs_tools.rs unless the grep in Step 1 finds actual references. The planner in the previous run hallucinated those as necessary targets — they are not confirmed to contain relevant content."}

{"notes":"## Postmortem (2026-03-23T21:45:11Z)

ROOT CAUSE: The TensorZero proxy does not have a routing entry for the function name "tensorzero::function_name::worker_code_edit", so every subtask worker immediately received a 502 Bad Gateway before doing any actual code editing. The baseline verification then confirmed the worktree was already broken (fmt failing), which caused the orchestrator to abort entirely rather than attempt recovery.

PATTERN: This is a TensorZero function-routing misconfiguration combined with a pre-existing baseline failure — the agent infrastructure was not ready to run before the coding work was even attempted. It is an infrastructure/config mismatch pattern, not a code-generation or file-targeting failure.

FIX STRATEGY:
- Before dispatching any subtasks, verify that the TensorZero gateway has a live route for "worker_code_edit" (and "cloud_manager_delegation") by hitting the TensorZero health or config endpoint; if the route is missing, add the function definition to tensorzero.toml (or equivalent config file) pointing at the desired cloud model, e.g. claude-sonnet-4-5 or gemini-3-flash-preview, before proceeding.
- Fix the pre-existing fmt failure in the worktree baseline — run "cargo fmt --all" in the repo root and commit the result so that 3/3 gates pass before any agent modifications begin; the orchestrator's baseline check will otherwise abort every future attempt on this branch.
- Once routing and baseline are green, the actual code changes are straightforward: add SWARM_OPENROUTER_URL and SWARM_OPENROUTER_API_KEY to crates/swarm-agents/src/config.rs, add a MiniMax M2.7 model constant and an openrouter provider arm in patches/rig-core-0.30.0/src/providers/openrouter/client.rs and completion.rs, and wire the new provider in patches/rig-core-0.30.0/src/client/builder.rs — these are all mechanical additions that do not require concurrent dispatch and should be done sequentially in a single worker to avoid merge conflicts.

REVISED DESCRIPTION:

Title: OpenRouter / MiniMax M2.7 integration

Priority: P2
Depends on: beefcake-uu4b-wko (Ensemble Swarm heterogeneous multi-model orchestration) — must be merged first.

PREREQUISITE CHECKS (must pass before any code edits):

1. Confirm TensorZero routing is healthy. The previous attempt failed because the function "tensorzero::function_name::worker_code_edit" had no registered provider. Before starting, check tensorzero.toml (location: typically repo root or infra/tensorzero/tensorzero.toml) and confirm a [functions.worker_code_edit] block exists with a valid model variant pointing at an available cloud model. If it is missing, add it. Do not proceed until a direct HTTP POST to the TensorZero gateway for that function returns 200.

2. Fix the pre-existing fmt failure. Run "cargo fmt --all" from the repo root, verify "cargo fmt --all -- --check" exits 0, and commit. The orchestrator baseline check requires fmt, clippy, and cargo check to all pass (3/3 gates) before it will allow agent modifications. The previous run showed 0/3 gates passing with fmt as the first failure.

IMPLEMENTATION — do these in order in a single sequential worker, not concurrent subtasks:

Step 1 — crates/swarm-agents/src/config.rs
- Add two new environment variable constants near the existing provider URL/key constants:
    pub const OPENROUTER_URL_ENV: &str = "SWARM_OPENROUTER_URL";
    pub const OPENROUTER_API_KEY_ENV: &str = "SWARM_OPENROUTER_API_KEY";
- Add a new field to the SwarmConfig struct (or equivalent top-level config struct):
    pub openrouter_url: Option<String>,
    pub openrouter_api_key: Option<String>,
- Populate those fields from std::env::var in the existing config loading function (look for the function that reads SWARM_CLOUD_API_KEY or similar and follow the same pattern).

Step 2 — patches/rig-core-0.30.0/src/providers/openrouter/client.rs
- Add a MiniMax M2.7 model constant at the top of the file alongside existing model constants:
    pub const MINIMAX_M2_7: &str = "minimax/minimax-m2.7";
- In the OpenRouterClient::new or equivalent constructor, ensure the base URL defaults to "https://openrouter.ai/api/v1" but can be overridden by the SWARM_OPENROUTER_URL env var (needed for testing/proxying).

Step 3 — patches/rig-core-0.30.0/src/providers/openrouter/completion.rs
- Confirm the CompletionModel implementation does not hard-code a model allowlist that would reject "minimax/minimax-m2.7". If there is a match or if-let on known model strings, add the MiniMax entry. If the provider is already open (passes model string through), no change is needed here.

Step 4 — patches/rig-core-0.30.0/src/client/builder.rs
- Add a new builder method, following the pattern of existing provider methods (e.g., with_anthropic or with_openai):
    pub fn with_openrouter(mut self, api_key: impl Into<String>) -> Self
- Inside that method, construct an OpenRouterClient using the api_key and the optional URL override from config, then register it in the provider map under the key "openrouter".
- Ensure the method is pub and returns Self for chaining.

Step 5 — integration wiring in crates/swarm-agents/src/agents.rs (or wherever cloud provider clients are instantiated)
- After loading SwarmConfig, if openrouter_api_key is Some, call .with_openrouter(key) on the client builder and log at INFO level: "OpenRouter provider registered model=minimax/minimax-m2.7".
- If the env var is absent, skip silently (do not error).

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- cargo fmt --all -- --check passes
- cargo clippy -- -D warnings passes
- cargo check passes
- SWARM_OPENROUTER_URL and SWARM_OPENROUTER_API_KEY are read from environment and wired to an OpenRouter client using the minimax/minimax-m2.7 model string
- If neither env var is set, the binary starts normally without panicking"}

{"notes":"## Postmortem (2026-03-23T21:48:53Z)

ROOT CAUSE: The TensorZero gateway had no registered routing entry for the function name "tensorzero::function_name::worker_code_edit", causing every subtask worker to receive a 502 Bad Gateway immediately upon first LLM call — before any file edits were attempted. Compounding this, the worktree baseline was already broken (cargo fmt failing) so even if the routing had been fixed mid-run, the orchestrator's escalation path would have aborted due to 0/3 gates passing.

PATTERN: This is an infrastructure/config mismatch pattern — the agent execution environment was not operational before the coding task was dispatched, not a code-generation, file-targeting, or compilation cascade failure.

FIX STRATEGY:
- Before dispatching any subtasks, verify the TensorZero gateway has a live route for "worker_code_edit" by inspecting tensorzero.toml (likely at repo root or infra/tensorzero/tensorzero.toml) for a [functions.worker_code_edit] block with a valid model variant; if missing, add it pointing at an available cloud model (e.g., claude-sonnet-4-5 or gemini-3-flash-preview) and confirm a direct HTTP POST to that function returns 200 before proceeding.
- Fix the pre-existing fmt failure by running "cargo fmt --all" from the repo root, verifying "cargo fmt --all -- --check" exits 0, and committing the result so that all 3 baseline gates (fmt, clippy, cargo check) pass before any agent modifications begin.
- Once routing and baseline are green, perform the code changes sequentially in a single worker (not concurrent subtasks) to avoid merge conflicts: add OPENROUTER_URL_ENV and OPENROUTER_API_KEY_ENV constants plus struct fields in crates/swarm-agents/src/config.rs, add the MINIMAX_M2_7 model constant and URL-override logic in patches/rig-core-0.30.0/src/providers/openrouter/client.rs, confirm no model allowlist rejection in patches/rig-core-0.30.0/src/providers/openrouter/completion.rs, add the with_openrouter builder method in patches/rig-core-0.30.0/src/client/builder.rs, and wire the conditional client registration in crates/swarm-agents/src/agents.rs.

REVISED DESCRIPTION:

Title: OpenRouter / MiniMax M2.7 integration

Priority: P2
Depends on: beefcake-uu4b-wko (must be merged first)

PREREQUISITE CHECKS — complete both before touching any source files:

Prerequisite 1 — TensorZero routing health
Open tensorzero.toml (check repo root first, then infra/tensorzero/tensorzero.toml). Search for a block beginning with [functions.worker_code_edit]. If it does not exist, add the following block:

  [functions.worker_code_edit]
  type = "chat"

  [functions.worker_code_edit.variants.cloud]
  type = "chat_completion"
  model = "claude-sonnet-4-5"
  weight = 1.0

Also confirm [functions.cloud_manager_delegation] exists with the same structure. After editing, restart the TensorZero gateway process and send a test HTTP POST to the gateway for function "worker_code_edit". Confirm the response is HTTP 200. Do not proceed until this passes.

Prerequisite 2 — baseline fmt fix
From the repo root of the worktree, run: cargo fmt --all
Then verify: cargo fmt --all -- --check (must exit 0)
Then verify: cargo clippy -- -D warnings (must exit 0)
Then verify: cargo check (must exit 0)
Commit the fmt fix with message "chore: cargo fmt baseline fix". The orchestrator baseline check requires all 3 gates to pass (3/3) before allowing agent modifications. The previous run showed 0/3 gates with fmt as the first failure, which caused the orchestrator to abort.

IMPLEMENTATION — execute all steps sequentially in a single worker, not as concurrent subtasks (concurrent dispatch caused merge conflicts in the previous dependency issue and is explicitly contraindicated here):

Step 1 — crates/swarm-agents/src/config.rs
Locate the block where existing provider URL and API key environment variable name constants are defined (search for SWARM_CLOUD_API_KEY or similar string constants near the top of the file). Immediately after those constants, add:

  pub const OPENROUTER_URL_ENV: &str = "SWARM_OPENROUTER_URL";
  pub const OPENROUTER_API_KEY_ENV: &str = "SWARM_OPENROUTER_API_KEY";

Locate the SwarmConfig struct (or equivalent top-level config struct). Add two new optional fields:

  pub openrouter_url: Option<String>,
  pub openrouter_api_key: Option<String>,

Locate the config loading function that calls std::env::var for existing provider keys (search for the function that reads SWARM_CLOUD_API_KEY). Following the exact same pattern used there, populate the new fields:

  openrouter_url: std::env::var(OPENROUTER_URL_ENV).ok(),
  openrouter_api_key: std::env::var(OPENROUTER_API_KEY_ENV).ok(),

If SwarmConfig derives or implements Default, ensure the new fields default to None.

Step 2 — patches/rig-core-0.30.0/src/providers/openrouter/client.rs
At the top of the file, alongside existing model string constants (search for pub const lines near the top), add:

  pub const MINIMAX_M2_7: &str = "minimax/minimax-m2.7";

Locate the OpenRouterClient::new constructor or equivalent initialization function. Confirm the base URL is set to "https://openrouter.ai/api/v1". If it is hard-coded as a literal, change it to read from the SWARM_OPENROUTER_URL environment variable with a fallback:

  let base_url = std::env::var("SWARM_OPENROUTER_URL")
      .unwrap_or_else(|_| "https://openrouter.ai/api/v1".to_string());

This allows the URL to be overridden in tests or when routing through a proxy.

Step 3 — patches/rig-core-0.30.0/src/providers/openrouter/completion.rs
Search the file for any match statement, if-let, or allowlist that checks the model string against a set of known model identifiers. If such a check exists and would reject "minimax/minimax-m2.7", add that string to the allowlist. If the provider passes the model string through without validation (the common case), no change is needed in this file — confirm by reading the CompletionModel::completion or equivalent method and verifying it uses self.model or a passed model string directly.

Step 4 — patches/rig-core-0.30.0/src/client/builder.rs
Locate an existing provider builder method such as with_anthropic or with_openai to use as a template. Following that exact pattern, add a new public method:

  pub fn with_openrouter(mut self, api_key: impl Into<String>) -> Self {
      let client = crate::providers::openrouter::OpenRouterClient::new(api_key.into());
      self.providers.insert("openrouter".to_string(), Box::new(client));
      self
  }

Adjust the body to match whatever pattern the existing with_anthropic or with_openai methods use for constructing and registering a provider — the key requirement is that the provider is registered under the string key "openrouter" and the method returns Self for chaining.

Step 5 — crates/swarm-agents/src/agents.rs (or wherever cloud provider clients are instantiated)
Locate the section that builds the client builder after loading SwarmConfig (search for .with_anthropic or .with_openai calls to find the right location). After the existing provider registrations, add a conditional block:

  if let Some(key) = &config.openrouter_api_key {
      builder = builder.with_openrouter(key.clone());
      tracing::info!(model = minimax_m2_7_constant, "OpenRouter provider registered");"}

{"notes":"## Postmortem (2026-03-23T21:56:20Z)

ROOT CAUSE: The worker subtasks all failed immediately with a 502 "unknown provider for model tensorzero::function_name::worker_code_edit" error, meaning the TensorZero gateway has no routing configuration for the worker_code_edit function — the workers never executed a single tool call. Compounding this, the baseline compilation check failed (fmt gate failed, clippy and check skipped), meaning the repo was already broken before any agent changes were attempted.

PATTERN: This is a dual infrastructure failure — TensorZero function misconfiguration (missing provider binding for worker_code_edit) combined with a pre-existing compilation/formatting failure in the worktree, neither of which is related to the feature logic itself.

FIX STRATEGY:
- Before re-running, verify the TensorZero gateway config has a valid provider entry for tensorzero::function_name::worker_code_edit (likely needs a cloud model binding such as claude-opus-4-6 or gemini-3-flash-preview mirroring the cloud_manager_delegation function); the gateway is running in cloud-only mode so local model bindings will not resolve.
- Run `cargo fmt --all` and `cargo clippy` manually in the worktree at commit 0b108c210092bd02333021406ebb53aecca6f38d to identify and fix the pre-existing formatting failure before the agent attempts any changes; the baseline must be green or the orchestrator will abort immediately after subtask failure.
- Once infrastructure is confirmed healthy, restructure the task as a single sequential agent pass rather than three concurrent subtasks, since the three files (mutation_archive.rs, work_packet/generator.rs, orchestrator/mod.rs) have a strict dependency order — UCB1 data structures must exist in mutation_archive.rs before generator.rs can call them, and orchestrator/mod.rs wiring must come last.

REVISED DESCRIPTION:

Title: Learning router: UCB1 model selection from mutation archive

Prerequisites before running:
1. Confirm TensorZero gateway has a provider binding for function tensorzero::function_name::worker_code_edit pointing to a cloud model (e.g., claude-opus-4-6). The system is running in cloud-only mode; without this binding all worker subtasks will 502 immediately.
2. Run `cargo fmt --all && cargo check` in the repo root and fix any pre-existing failures. The baseline must pass all three gates (fmt, clippy, check) before the agent starts or the orchestrator will abort.

Depends on: beefcake-uu4b-wko (ensemble swarm) and beefcake-uu4b-zij (phase-based model selector). Both are merged. The PhaseModelSelector struct and ModelPhase enum from beefcake-uu4b-zij are available in crates/swarm-agents/src/phase_selector.rs.

Implementation steps (execute in this order — do not parallelize):

Step 1 — Add UCB1 data structures and logic to crates/swarm-agents/src/mutation_archive.rs:
- Add a new struct Ucb1Arm with fields: model_id: String, task_type: String, successes: u64, failures: u64, total_pulls: u64.
- Add a method fn ucb1_score(&self, total_rounds: u64, exploration_constant: f64) -> f64 implementing the standard UCB1 formula: mean_reward + exploration_constant * sqrt(ln(total_rounds) / self.total_pulls). Handle the zero-pulls case by returning f64::INFINITY so unvisited arms are always explored first.
- Add a struct LearningRouter containing a HashMap<(String, String), Ucb1Arm> keyed by (model_id, task_type).
- Implement fn select_model(router: &LearningRouter, task_type: &str, candidates: &[String]) -> String that iterates candidates, looks up or initializes arms, computes ucb1_score for each, and returns the model_id with the highest score.
- Implement fn record_outcome(router: &mut LearningRouter, model_id: &str, task_type: &str, success: bool) that increments successes or failures and total_pulls on the matching arm.
- Derive serde Serialize/Deserialize on all new structs so state can be persisted to the mutation archive Dolt table.

Step 2 — Integrate UCB1 selection into coordination/src/work_packet/generator.rs:
- Import LearningRouter and select_model from swarm_agents::mutation_archive.
- The WorkPacketGenerator struct already holds a model selection field; add an optional field ucb1_router: Option<Arc<Mutex<LearningRouter>>>.

1. Confirm TensorZero gateway has a provider binding for function tensorzero::function_name::worker_code_edit pointing to a cloud model (e.g., claude-opus-4-6). The system is running in cloud-only mode; without this binding all worker subtasks will 502 immediately.

{"notes":"## Postmortem (2026-03-23T21:59:47Z)

ROOT CAUSE: Two independent infrastructure failures blocked all execution before any code was written: (1) the TensorZero gateway has no provider binding for tensorzero::function_name::worker_code_edit, causing every worker subtask to 502 immediately with zero tool calls made, and (2) the worktree at commit 0b108c210092bd02333021406ebb53aecca6f38d already fails cargo fmt, causing the baseline gate to abort the session after the subtask failures were detected.

PATTERN: Dual infrastructure failure — missing gateway function binding combined with a pre-existing compilation/formatting failure in the worktree, neither caused by agent logic; the agent never touched a single file.

FIX STRATEGY:
- Add a provider binding for tensorzero::function_name::worker_code_edit in the TensorZero gateway config pointing to a cloud model (claude-opus-4-6 or gemini-3-flash-preview, matching the cloud_manager_delegation binding); the system is in cloud-only mode so any local model reference will fail to resolve.
- Manually run cargo fmt --all in the worktree at commit 0b108c210092bd02333021406ebb53aecca6f38d, commit the formatting fix, and confirm all three gates (fmt, clippy, check) pass green before re-queuing the issue; the orchestrator will abort immediately if the baseline is red.
- Restructure the task as a single sequential agent pass rather than three concurrent subtasks, because mutation_archive.rs must define Ucb1Arm and LearningRouter before generator.rs can import them, and orchestrator/mod.rs wiring must come last; concurrent dispatch of these three files will produce import-not-found compile errors even if the gateway is healthy.

REVISED DESCRIPTION:

Title: Learning router: UCB1 model selection from mutation archive

Prerequisites (must be verified by a human operator before re-queuing, not by the agent):

1. TensorZero gateway config must have a valid provider entry for the function tensorzero::function_name::worker_code_edit bound to a cloud model such as claude-opus-4-6. The system runs in cloud-only mode; any missing or local-only binding produces a 502 on the first LLM call and the worker exits with zero tool calls. Confirm by curling the gateway health endpoint and checking that worker_code_edit resolves before starting the run.

2. Run the following in the repo root (not the worktree) and fix all output before re-queuing:
   cargo fmt --all
   cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings
   cargo check --all-targets
   All three must exit 0. The worktree is branched from commit 0b108c210092bd02333021406ebb53aecca6f38d which currently fails the fmt gate; the orchestrator will skip the issue immediately if the baseline is red.

3. Do not parallelize the three implementation steps. The files have a strict dependency order. The agent must execute them sequentially in one pass.

Depends on: beefcake-uu4b-wko (ensemble swarm, merged) and beefcake-uu4b-zij (phase-based model selector, merged). PhaseModelSelector and ModelPhase are available in crates/swarm-agents/src/phase_selector.rs.

Implementation steps — execute in this exact order, one at a time, do not start step 2 until step 1 compiles:

Step 1 — crates/swarm-agents/src/mutation_archive.rs

Add the following to the existing file. Do not replace the file; append or insert into the appropriate module section.

Add struct Ucb1Arm with fields:
  model_id: String
  task_type: String
  successes: u64
  failures: u64
  total_pulls: u64
Derive serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize, Debug, Clone on Ucb1Arm.

Add method on Ucb1Arm:
  pub fn ucb1_score(&self, total_rounds: u64, exploration_constant: f64) -> f64
  If self.total_pulls == 0, return f64::INFINITY.
  Otherwise return (self.successes as f64 / self.total_pulls as f64) + exploration_constant * ((total_rounds as f64).ln() / self.total_pulls as f64).sqrt()

Add struct LearningRouter:
  arms: HashMap<(String, String), Ucb1Arm>
  total_rounds: u64
Derive serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize, Debug, Clone, Default on LearningRouter.

Add free function:
  pub fn select_model(router: &LearningRouter, task_type: &str, candidates: &[String]) -> String
  For each candidate in candidates, look up the arm keyed by (candidate.clone(), task_type.to_string()). If the arm does not exist, treat its score as f64::INFINITY (unvisited). Return the candidate with the highest ucb1_score(router.total_rounds, 2.0_f64.sqrt()). If candidates is empty, panic with "select_model called with empty candidates list".

Add free function:
  pub fn record_outcome(router: &mut LearningRouter, model_id: &str, task_type: &str, success: bool)
  Get or insert the arm for key (model_id.to_string(), task_type.to_string()), initializing all counts to 0 and copying model_id and task_type strings if inserting. Increment total_pulls by 1. If success is true, increment successes by 1, otherwise increment failures by 1. Increment router.total_rounds by 1.

Add unit test module at the bottom of the file:
  #[cfg(test)]
  mod ucb1_tests {
    Test name: after_10_successes_for_a_and_2_for_b_select_returns_b
    Create an empty LearningRouter.
    Call record_outcome 10 times with model_id="model_a", task_type="code_edit", success=true.
    Call record_outcome 2 times with model_id="model_b", task_type="code_edit", success=true.
    Call select_model with task_type="code_edit" and candidates=["model_a".to_string(), "model_b".to_string()].
    Assert the result equals "model_b". Model B has fewer pulls so its UCB1 exploration bonus dominates.
  }

After completing step 1, run cargo check -p swarm-agents and confirm it exits 0 before proceeding.

Step 2 — coordination/src/work_packet/generator.rs

Read the existing file first with the read_file tool before making any edits.

Add to imports at the top:
  use swarm_agents::mutation_archive::{LearningRouter, select_model, record_outcome};
  use std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};

In the WorkPacketGenerator struct, add one field:
  ucb1_router: Option<Arc<Mutex<LearningRouter>>>

In the generate() method, locate the section where the model string is finalized. After task_type is determined and before the model string is written to the work packet, add a block:
  if let Some(router_arc) = &self.ucb1_router {
      let router = router_arc.lock().expect("ucb1_router lock poisoned");
      let candidates: Vec<String> = /* collect the existing candidate model list from whatever the phase selector produces */;
      if !candidates.is_empty() {
          selected_model = select_model(&router, &task_type, &candidates);
      }
  }
  The phase selector result must remain the fallback when ucb1_router is None.

In the work packet completion callback (wherever success or failure is reported back to the generator), add:
  if let Some(router_arc) = &self.ucb1_router {
      let mut router = router_arc.lock().expect("ucb1_router lock poisoned");
      record_outcome(&mut router, &used_model, &task_type, success);
  }

After completing step 2, run cargo check -p coordination and confirm it exits 0 before proceeding.

Step 3 — crates/swarm-agents/src/orchestrator/mod.rs

Read the existing file first with the read_file tool"}

{"notes":"## Postmortem (2026-03-23T22:03:24Z)

ROOT CAUSE: Two independent infrastructure failures blocked all execution before any code was written: (1) the TensorZero gateway has no provider binding for tensorzero::function_name::worker_code_edit, causing every worker subtask to 502 immediately with zero tool calls made, and (2) the worktree at commit 0b108c210092bd02333021406ebb53aecca6f38d already fails cargo fmt, causing the baseline gate to abort the session after the subtask failures were detected.

PATTERN: Dual infrastructure failure — missing gateway function binding combined with a pre-existing formatting failure in the worktree, neither caused by agent logic; the agent never touched a single file.

FIX STRATEGY:
- Add a provider binding for tensorzero::function_name::worker_code_edit in the TensorZero gateway config pointing to a cloud model (claude-opus-4-6 or gemini-3-flash-preview, matching the cloud_manager_delegation binding); the system is in cloud-only mode so any local model reference will fail to resolve. Verify by curling the gateway health endpoint before re-queuing.
- Manually run cargo fmt --all in the worktree at commit 0b108c210092bd02333021406ebb53aecca6f38d, commit the formatting fix, and confirm all three gates (fmt, clippy, check) exit 0 before re-queuing; the orchestrator aborts immediately if the baseline is red and will not attempt any agent work.
- Restructure the task as a single sequential agent pass rather than three concurrent subtasks, because mutation_archive.rs must define Ucb1Arm and LearningRouter before generator.rs can import them, and orchestrator/mod.rs wiring must come last; concurrent dispatch of these three files will produce import-not-found compile errors even if the gateway is healthy.

REVISED DESCRIPTION:

Title: Learning router: UCB1 model selection from mutation archive

Prerequisites — a human operator must verify all three before re-queuing, not the agent:

1. TensorZero gateway config must have a valid provider entry for the function tensorzero::function_name::worker_code_edit bound to a cloud model such as claude-opus-4-6. The system runs in cloud-only mode; any missing or local-only binding produces a 502 on the first LLM call and the worker exits with zero tool calls. Confirm by curling the gateway health endpoint and checking that worker_code_edit resolves before starting the run.

2. Run the following in the repo root and fix all output before re-queuing:
   cargo fmt --all
   cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings
   cargo check --all-targets
   All three must exit 0. The worktree is branched from commit 0b108c210092bd02333021406ebb53aecca6f38d which currently fails the fmt gate; the orchestrator will skip the issue immediately if the baseline is red.

3. Do not parallelize the three implementation steps. The files have a strict dependency order. The agent must execute them sequentially in one pass, verifying cargo check -p <crate> after each step before proceeding to the next.

Depends on: beefcake-uu4b-wko (ensemble swarm, merged) and beefcake-uu4b-zij (phase-based model selector, merged). PhaseModelSelector and ModelPhase are available in crates/swarm-agents/src/phase_selector.rs.

Implementation steps — execute in this exact order, one at a time, do not start step 2 until step 1 compiles:

Step 1 — crates/swarm-agents/src/mutation_archive.rs

Read the existing file first with the read_file tool before making any edits. Do not replace the file; append or insert into the appropriate module section.

Add struct Ucb1Arm with fields:
  model_id: String
  task_type: String
  successes: u64
  failures: u64
  total_pulls: u64
Derive serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize, Debug, Clone on Ucb1Arm.

Add method on Ucb1Arm:
  pub fn ucb1_score(&self, total_rounds: u64, exploration_constant: f64) -> f64
  If self.total_pulls == 0, return f64::INFINITY.
  Otherwise return (self.successes as f64 / self.total_pulls as f64) + exploration_constant * ((total_rounds as f64).ln() / self.total_pulls as f64).sqrt()

Add struct LearningRouter:
  arms: HashMap<(String, String), Ucb1Arm>
  total_rounds: u64
Derive serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize, Debug, Clone, Default on LearningRouter.

Add free function:
  pub fn select_model(router: &LearningRouter, task_type: &str, candidates: &[String]) -> String
  For each candidate in candidates, look up the arm keyed by (candidate.clone(), task_type.to_string()). If the arm does not exist, treat its score as f64::INFINITY (unvisited). Return the candidate with the highest ucb1_score(router.total_rounds, 2.0_f64.sqrt()). If candidates is empty, panic with "select_model called with empty candidates list".

Add free function:
  pub fn record_outcome(router: &mut LearningRouter, model_id: &str, task_type: &str, success: bool)
  Get or insert the arm for key (model_id.to_string(), task_type.to_string()), initializing all counts to 0 and copying model_id and task_type strings if inserting. Increment total_pulls by 1. If success is true, increment successes by 1, otherwise increment failures by 1. Increment router.total_rounds by 1.

Add unit test module at the bottom of the file:
  #[cfg(test)]
  mod ucb1_tests {
    Test name: after_10_successes_for_a_and_2_for_b_select_returns_b
    Create an empty LearningRouter::default().
    Call record_outcome 10 times with model_id="model_a", task_type="code_edit", success=true.
    Call record_outcome 2 times with model_id="model_b", task_type="code_edit", success=true.
    Call select_model with task_type="code_edit" and candidates=vec!["model_a".to_string(), "model_b".to_string()].
    Assert the result equals "model_b". Model B has fewer pulls so its UCB1 exploration bonus dominates.
  }

After completing step 1, run cargo check -p swarm-agents and confirm it exits 0 before proceeding to step 2. If it does not exit 0, fix all errors before moving on.

Step 2 — coordination/src/work_packet/generator.rs

Read the existing file first with the read_file tool before making any edits.

Add to imports at the top:
  use swarm_agents::mutation_archive::{LearningRouter, select_model, record_outcome};
  use std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};

In the WorkPacketGenerator struct, add one field:
  ucb1_router: Option<Arc<Mutex<LearningRouter>>>

In the generate() method, locate the section where the model string is finalized. After task_type is determined and before the model string is written to the work packet, add a block:
  if let Some(router_arc) = &self.ucb1_router {
      let router = router_arc.lock().expect("ucb1_router lock poisoned");
      let candidates: Vec<String> = collect the existing candidate model list from whatever the phase selector produces;
      if !candidates.is_empty() {
          selected_model = select_model(&router, &task_type, &candidates);
      }
  }
  The phase selector result must remain the fallback when ucb1_router is None.

In the work packet completion callback (wherever success or failure is reported back to the generator), add:
  if let Some(router_arc) = &self.ucb1_router {
      let mut router = router_arc.lock().expect("ucb1_router lock poisoned");
      record_outcome(&mut router, &used_model, &task_type, success"}

  use swarm_agents::mutation_archive::{LearningRouter, select_model, record_outcome};
  use std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};

In the WorkPacketGenerator struct, add one field:
  ucb1_router: Option<Arc<Mutex<LearningRouter>>>

  if let Some(router_arc) = &self.ucb1_router {

      }
  }

  if let Some(router_arc) = &self.ucb1_router {

{"notes":"## Postmortem (2026-03-23T22:07:04Z)

ROOT CAUSE: All three subtask workers failed immediately with a 502 Bad Gateway error because TensorZero has no registered provider for the function "tensorzero::function_name::worker_code_edit" — the worker routing table is missing this function definition entirely. The baseline verification also failed independently because the worktree did not compile before any agent modifications, meaning there is a pre-existing fmt/clippy failure in the repo that must be resolved first.

PATTERN: This is a dual infrastructure failure — a missing TensorZero function registration (routing misconfiguration) combined with a pre-existing compilation/format failure in the baseline worktree, neither of which is related to the logic of the feature being implemented.

FIX STRATEGY:
- Before retrying, register the "worker_code_edit" function in the TensorZero configuration (tensorzero.toml or equivalent gateway config) with a valid cloud provider variant (e.g., claude-opus-4-6 or gemini-3-flash-preview) so the 502 does not recur; confirm with a direct curl to the TensorZero gateway that the function resolves.
- Run "cargo fmt --all" and "cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features" in the main repo (not the worktree) and commit the fixes before the next attempt, so the baseline verification passes 3/3 gates and the agent is not skipped immediately.
- Scope the implementation to three specific, well-bounded edits: (1) add a RiskLevel enum and ConsensusGate struct with a vote() method to coordination/src/debate/consensus.rs, (2) add a consensus_required field and pending_votes map to the DebateState in coordination/src/debate/state.rs, and (3) add a risk_check() call at the top of the apply_change handler in crates/swarm-agents/src/tools/exec_tool.rs that gates execution on ConsensusGate::quorum_reached() when RiskLevel is High.

REVISED DESCRIPTION:

Title: Consensus voting for critical changes

Context: This feature requires two dependencies that are already merged: the heterogeneous multi-model orchestration (beefcake-uu4b-wko) and the phase-based model selector (beefcake-uu4b-zij). Before the agent starts, a human operator must (a) register the "worker_code_edit" function in the TensorZero gateway config and restart the gateway, and (b) run "cargo fmt --all && cargo clippy --all-targets" in the main repo and commit the result so the baseline compiles clean.

Goal: When a proposed change is classified as high-risk (touches security-related code, modifies a public API surface, or diffs across more than 8 files), the system must solicit independent assessments from at least two distinct models and require a quorum before the change is applied.

Step 1 — coordination/src/debate/consensus.rs:
Add a RiskLevel enum with variants Low, Medium, High. Add a ConsensusGate struct that holds a required_votes: usize field (set to 2 for High risk), a votes: Vec<ModelVote> field where ModelVote carries model_id: String and approve: bool, and a timestamp. Implement fn cast_vote(&mut self, model_id: String, approve: bool) and fn quorum_reached(&self) -> bool that returns true when the count of approve==true votes is >= required_votes. Implement fn assess_risk(file_count: usize, touches_security: bool, touches_public_api: bool) -> RiskLevel that returns High when any of the three conditions is met (file_count > 8, touches_security, touches_public_api), Medium for moderate changes, and Low otherwise. Export all three types from coordination/src/debate/mod.rs.

Step 2 — coordination/src/debate/state.rs:
Add two fields to the existing DebateState struct: consensus_gate: Option<ConsensusGate> (None when risk is Low or Medium) and risk_level: RiskLevel (default Low). Add a method fn initialize_consensus(&mut self, file_count: usize, touches_security: bool, touches_public_api: bool) that calls assess_risk, sets self.risk_level, and if the result is High sets self.consensus_gate to Some(ConsensusGate::new(2)). Do not change any existing fields or method signatures.

Step 3 — crates/swarm-agents/src/tools/exec_tool.rs:
In the apply_change function (or equivalent entry point that writes files and runs commands), add a call to state.initialize_consensus(file_count, touches_security, touches_public_api) before any file writes. Then add a guard: if let Some(gate) = &state.consensus_gate { if !gate.quorum_reached() { return Err(ConsensusRequired { risk_level: state.risk_level.clone(), votes_so_far: gate.votes.len(), required: gate.required_votes }); } }. The ConsensusRequired error type should be added to the existing error enum in that crate. Do not modify the exec_tool's shell execution path or timeout logic.

Verification: After implementation, "cargo fmt --all", "cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings", and "cargo check --all" must all pass. Add a unit test in coordination/src/debate/consensus.rs that asserts quorum_reached() is false with one vote and true with two approving votes, and that assess_risk(9, false, false) returns High."}
